source_jobs.title: Spontaneous application
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SONIO



Each year, 140M children are born. Birth defects affect 1 in 33 births in developed countries, and in 50% of cases, they are not detected during prenatal examinations. Prenatal medicine is particularly complex, and the scans impose heavy responsibilities on healthcare professionals. They can also be a source of stress for future parents.



Resulting from 5 years of collaboration between world-renowned experts from Necker Hospital and Ecole Polytechnique, Sonio uses artificial intelligence to improve prenatal screening and diagnosis. Based on patented algorithms and a proprietary expert database, Sonio aims to become the reference tool to help practitioners improve screening, reduce diagnostic errors, and optimize pregnant women’s medical path.



Sonio's mission is to improve women's and children's health by promoting access and quality of care through technological innovation, medical expertise, and collective intelligence.



We're always looking for new talents interested in having an impact on Women's health.



Please apply with a short text on what you're looking for!



We review manually all spontaneous application :)



We move fast and aspire to be transparent over the process - our objective is that the process from the first chat to an offer is no longer than a month. We also aspire to give an answer to every application in a week - if you have not heard from us, please follow up at careers@sonio.ai.

source_jobs.title: Senior Clinical & AI Product Manager (Korean Speaking - relocation support available)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SONIO



Each year, 140M children are born. Birth defects affect 1 in 33 births in developed countries, and in 50% of cases, they are not detected during prenatal examinations. Prenatal medicine is particularly complex, and the scans impose heavy responsibilities on healthcare professionals. They can also be a source of stress for future parents.



Resulting from 5 years of collaboration between world-renowned experts from Necker Hospital and Ecole Polytechnique, Sonio uses artificial intelligence to improve prenatal screening and diagnosis. Based on patented algorithms and a proprietary expert database, Sonio aims to become the reference tool to help practitioners improve screening, reduce diagnostic errors, and optimize pregnant women’s medical path.



Sonio's mission is to improve women's and children's health by promoting access and quality of care through technological innovation, medical expertise, and collective intelligence.



About the role



We are looking for an experienced product manager who can drive the development and adoption of anomaly detection of AI features for the Maternal & Fetal Medicine (MFM) market

You’ll join a newly created team that will work on synergy projects between Sonio and Samsung. You’ll work on advanced technological projects mixing both hardware and software with cutting-edge AI/ML.



Key Responsibilities:

 - Product strategy & roadmap: Own the end-to-end lifecycle of AI features related to the detection & management of in-the-machine fetal abnormalities in-utero. This includes defining the long-term vision and prioritising features based on clinical need, market trends, technical feasibility & business impact.

 - Clinical & Market Intelligence: Work closely with healthcare providers, key opinion leaders, insurance providers, and patients to identify unmet needs and pain points.

 - Cross-functional leadership: Act as glue between engineering, design, regulatory, and clinical affairs translating complex medical requirements into actionable technical & clinical specifications that can be implemented by organization.

 - Create Product Specification and Technical Verification
   
   - Create product specifications, focusing on algorithm, performance, and UX-related aspects.
   
   - Iteratively test with clinicians, analyze feedback, and propose improvements for product performance.

 - Product Verification and Quality Assurance
   
   - Comprehensively verify, via in-house as well as field testing, whether the product meets intended specifications and clinical requirements in terms of functionality, performance, and UX.
   
   - Identify and prioritize key issues, and promote resolution through collaboration with the development team.

 - Data Analysis and Product Value Assessment
   
   - Define and monitor product performance KPIs.
   
   - Analyse user personas and derive market insights.
   
   - Propose data-driven product improvements.

 - Customer and KOL Operations
   
   - Establish demo and clinical test plans, and coordinate schedules/communication.
   
   - Prepare test environments and perform on-site demos.
   
   - Collect and follow up on feedback from customers and KOLs.

 - Commercialisation: Work closely with sales and marketing to develop a go-to-market strategy, pricing, and clinical materials and evidence for driving product adoption

This is a critical role with high exposure to both Samsung Healthcare and Sonio’s leadership. You'll have a huge impact on how we successfully



About the profile



 - You have at least 5+ years of product experience in a relevant context

 - You have some understanding of AI & Healthcare/Clinical products through previous experience

 - You are very analytical: you master SQL & data visualisation tools, reporting, UX KPIs, heatmaps, funnels, etc.

 - You value teamwork, and you are team-oriented. This role will interact with most of the teams internally. Moreover, happiness and well-being are a priority in the way we’re

source_jobs.title: Staff Engineer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SONIO



Each year, 140M children are born. Birth defects affect 1 in 33 births in developed countries, and in 50% of cases, they are not detected during prenatal examinations. Prenatal medicine is particularly complex, and the scans impose heavy responsibilities on healthcare professionals. They can also be a source of stress for future parents.



Resulting from 5 years of collaboration between world-renowned experts from Necker Hospital and Ecole Polytechnique, Sonio uses artificial intelligence to improve prenatal screening and diagnosis. Based on patented algorithms and a proprietary expert database, Sonio aims to become the reference tool to help practitioners improve screening, reduce diagnostic errors, and optimize pregnant women’s medical path.



Sonio's mission is to improve women's and children's health by promoting access and quality of care through technological innovation, medical expertise, and collective intelligence.



About the role



As a Staff Engineer at Sonio, you will be the ultimate technical authority for our Elixir/Phoenix ecosystem. You will join one of our squads led by Romain (Engineering Manager), which houses the core of our backend codebase.

In this role, you will architect high-scale, resilient systems and arbitrate complex technical decisions to ensure the platform remains performant as we grow. You act as a force multiplier, driving engineering velocity across your squad through strategic design, clear communication, and deep-tier problem-solving.



Your main missions:

 - Be the in-house Elixir / Phoenix expert squads rely on for the deepest problems

 - Drive architecture for scale: concurrency, fault tolerance, clustering, back-pressure, recovery patterns

 - Own the hardest problems end-to-end—from scoping and design to implementation—while distributing knowledge across the team.

 - Author RFCs / ADRs for cross-squad changes and review others' designs

 - Drive the refactors and tech-debt reductions that unlock velocity

 - Grow the team through pairing, code reviews, design reviews and internal talks

 - Contribute to senior / staff hiring (case design, tech deep-dives)

You'll join a growing engineering team with real room to shape how we build.





About the profile



Sonio is a mission-driven company, so interest in our mission is critical. Other requirements are:

 - 8+ years of software engineering, with 3+ years in Elixir in production at scale (real bottlenecks debugged)

 - You have expertise in web & cloud native environment,

 - Deep BEAM mastery: GenServers, Phoenix, Ecto / PostgreSQL performance

 - Distributed systems in a cloud context: fault tolerance, clustering, consistency/availability trade-offs, back-pressure, and recovery

 - Strong architecture and API design; comfortable with event-driven, data-intensive systems

 - Written communication is strong enough to author RFCs / ADRs that others use as a reference

 - Technical leadership without authority: drives consensus across squads, mentors peers

 - Fluent English; French a plus

 - Bonus: experience in a regulated environment (medical devices, fintech…), React / TypeScript familiarity



Location: We have offices in Paris, but this position can work remotely (ability to come to Paris every 1–1.5 months)



Salary: €85k – €100k + 10% bonus

We move fast and aspire to be transparent over the process - our objective is that the process from the first chat to an offer is no longer than a month. We also aspire to give an answer to every application in a week - if you have not heard from us, please follow up at careers@sonio.ai.

source_jobs.title: Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SONIO



Each year, 140M children are born. Birth defects affect 1 in 33 births in developed countries, and in 50% of cases, they are not detected during prenatal examinations. Prenatal medicine is particularly complex, and the scans impose heavy responsibilities on healthcare professionals. They can also be a source of stress for future parents.



Resulting from 5 years of collaboration between world-renowned experts from Necker Hospital and Ecole Polytechnique, Sonio uses artificial intelligence to improve prenatal screening and diagnosis. Based on patented algorithms and a proprietary expert database, Sonio aims to become the reference tool to help practitioners improve screening, reduce diagnostic errors, and optimize pregnant women’s medical path.



Sonio's mission is to improve women's and children's health by promoting access and quality of care through technological innovation, medical expertise, and collective intelligence.



ABOUT THE ROLE



This role is a chance to work on a high-impact, clinically meaningful product: you will contribute directly to the detection of severe fetal anomalies. You will join a cross-functional team and have real influence on what ships and how it performs.

You will own the end-to-end delivery of AI components, from data curation and model training to evaluation, error analysis, and iterative improvements in production.

You will also join at a pivotal moment in our algorithm development, with the opportunity to take a project from value definition all the way to day-to-day use by clinicians.




ABOUT THE PROFILE



Sonio is a mission-driven company — genuine interest in our mission and in meaningful clinical AI applications is essential. We’re looking for someone who is hands-on, curious, and eager to learn fast. Requirements:

 - 1-2 years of professional experience (including internships) is ideal, but we’re open to strong candidates who feel this role is a good fit.

 - Practical ML experience: you can implement, run, and iterate quickly on AI/ML components (not only design ideas). Experience with medical imaging and/or computer vision is a big plus.

 - Strong Python skills: you follow good practices, write clean code, and are comfortable writing tests. SQL is a plus (or strong willingness to learn).

 - Strong analytical and debugging instincts: you can identify what drives model performance, run solid error analysis, and propose a clear action plan.

 - Clear communication: you can explain technical results, risks, and limitations to non-technical stakeholders (e.g. product, clinicians).

 - Comfortable with ambiguity and autonomy; you take initiative and drive work forward.

 - Master’s degree (minimum) in AI, Computer Vision, Biomedical Engineering (or equivalent proven experience).

 - Bonus: experience with AI coding tools (e.g. Claude Code) or motivation to adopt them quickly.



Localisation: Paris office or remote in France

Salary: €45k - €55k + 10%



We move fast and aspire to be transparent over the process - our objective is that the process from the first chat to an offer is no longer than a month. We also aspire to give an answer to every application in a week - if you have not heard from us, please follow up at careers@sonio.ai.

source_jobs.title: Part-time MFM Medical Expert
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SONIO



Each year, 140M children are born. Birth defects affect 1 in 33 births in developed countries, and in 50% of cases, they are not detected during prenatal examinations. Prenatal medicine is particularly complex, and the scans impose heavy responsibilities on healthcare professionals. They can also be a source of stress for future parents.



Resulting from 5 years of collaboration between world-renowned experts from Necker Hospital and Ecole Polytechnique, Sonio uses artificial intelligence to improve prenatal screening and diagnosis. Based on patented algorithms and a proprietary expert database, Sonio aims to become the reference tool to help practitioners improve screening, reduce diagnostic errors, and optimize pregnant women’s medical path.



Sonio's mission is to improve women's and children's health by promoting access and quality of care through technological innovation, medical expertise, and collective intelligence.



About the opportunity



We are looking for US-based MFM passionate about advancing fetal medicine through AI to bring expertise to our building, the next generation of AI software at Sonio

We are looking for someone who can contribute at least 1 day a week, with some flexibility, and who still maintains a clinical practice.

You'll be joining the team that is actively building anomaly detection, you'll join their weekly rituals and be fully part of the team, not just acting as an external advisor.



About the profile


Ideally, we are looking for:

 - MD and MFM board-certified, and ideally either FACOG or FAIUM certification

 - You are based in the US with a strong understanding of the US prenatal healthcare landscape

 - You have at least some clinical experience in private practice, and ideally in various set-ups, including remote reading

 - You have experience with software, ideally AI (e.g., purchasing it, rolling it out, etc.).

 - Strong plus if you already have experience as a clinical advisor for a software team
   
   

We move fast and aspire to be transparent over the process - our objective is that the process from the first chat to an offer is no longer than a month. We also aspire to give an answer to every application in a week - if you have not heard from us, please follow up at careers@sonio.ai.

source_jobs.title: Growth Marketing Intern
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SONIO



Each year, 140M children are born. Birth defects affect 1 in 33 births in developed countries, and in 50% of cases, they are not detected during prenatal examinations. Prenatal medicine is particularly complex, and the scans impose heavy responsibilities on healthcare professionals. They can also be a source of stress for future parents.



Resulting from 5 years of collaboration between world-renowned experts from Necker Hospital and Ecole Polytechnique, Sonio uses artificial intelligence to improve prenatal screening and diagnosis. Based on patented algorithms and a proprietary expert database, Sonio aims to become the reference tool to help practitioners improve screening, reduce diagnostic errors, and optimize pregnant women’s medical path.



Sonio's mission is to improve women's and children's health by promoting access and quality of care through technological innovation, medical expertise, and collective intelligence.





About the role



As a Marketing Intern at Sonio, you will report to the Growth Marketing Manager and collaborate closely with Brand, Product Marketing, and Sales. You’ll get hands-on experience shaping Sonio’s growth marketing strategy and launching high-impact campaigns tailored to the US healthcare market. You’ll also have the opportunity to dive deeper into analytics and revenue operations, learning how to track performance and contribute to our revenue pipeline.



 - Account-Based Marketing (ABM): You’ll help identify, research, and target high-value healthcare accounts in the US market. You will assist in building personalized content assets, orchestrating outreach campaigns, and aligning with the Sales team to engage key decision-makers at target hospitals and clinics.

 - Growth Strategy: You’ll work alongside the Growth Marketing Manager to analyze and optimize the end-to-end prospect journey. You will help brainstorm tactics to generate new leads, develop experiments to improve conversion rates, and contribute actionable ideas to shape how we expand our footprint in the US healthcare market.

 - Analytics and reporting: You’ll help monitor marketing and sales KPIs and work on insightful dashboards, empowering our sales and marketing teams to make data-driven decisions.

 - CRM management: You’ll act as a co-pilot for our HubSpot CRM system, ensuring our marketing campaigns and sales activities run smoothly. You will assist in cleaning and organizing lead data, segmenting target lists for marketing campaigns, and ensuring accurate tracking of prospects as they move through the funnel.





About the profile





Sonio is a mission-driven company, so interest in our mission is critical. Other requirements are:

 - You are comfortable with numbers: you can look beyond raw data to see the story and meaning behind it, translating metrics into actionable insights.

 - You have great organizational skills and a sharp eye for detail.

 - You are a strong, collaborative communicator who can convey ideas clearly and concisely.

 - You are highly proficient in Excel or Google Sheets and actively use AI tools to streamline your workflows. Any previous hands-on experience with HubSpot and with data visualization tools would be a major plus.

 - You are bilingual or possess professional-level fluency in English.



Start date: ASAP for 6 months

Location: Paris

We move fast and aspire to be transparent over the process - our objective is that the process from the first chat to an offer is no longer than a month. We also aspire to give an answer to every application in a week - if you have not heard from us, please follow up at careers@sonio.ai.

source_jobs.title: Regulatory Affairs Internship/Alternance
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SONIO



Each year, 140M children are born. Birth defects affect 1 in 33 births in developed countries, and in 50% of cases, they are not detected during prenatal examinations. Prenatal medicine is particularly complex, and the scans impose heavy responsibilities on healthcare professionals. They can also be a source of stress for future parents.



Resulting from 5 years of collaboration between world-renowned experts from Necker Hospital and Ecole Polytechnique, Sonio uses artificial intelligence to improve prenatal screening and diagnosis. Based on patented algorithms and a proprietary expert database, Sonio aims to become the reference tool to help practitioners improve screening, reduce diagnostic errors, and optimize pregnant women’s medical path.



Sonio's mission is to improve women's and children's health by promoting access and quality of care through technological innovation, medical expertise, and collective intelligence.





About the role



You will join our RAQA team and be attached to Zahra, Senior RAQA Manager.

Our team makes miracles happen and ensures our products are FDA-cleared, often in record time. During this experience, you’ll be at the heart of this team and learn how RA works in a tech startup.

Your role will be to support our team by continuously improving internal processes, ensuring regulatory compliance, and assisting with regulatory submission deliverables.

Key missions:

 - Monitor and ensure the timely preparation and tracking of technical documentation related to design changes, in coordination with the relevant teams

 - Assist in documentation preparation and publishing for regulatory submissions

 - Assist in implementing an automated system for the regulatory and normative watch process

 - Proactively monitor and ensure timely renewal of licenses, including Small Business Determination, Device Listing, and Establishment License for the US market

 - Facilitate promotional marketing review by setting up a centralized database of validated marketing and clinical claims

 - Perform regulatory watch to ensure compliance with FDA regulations, ISO standards, and other relevant regulatory requirements and provide support for regulatory impact analysis

 - Provide support for Post-market compliance activities





About the profile



Sonio is a mission-driven company so interest in our mission is critical. Other requirements are:

 - Ongoing Bac+5 in the medical devices field in preference (engineering degree, master's in regulatory affairs…)

 - Knowledge in medical devices, and software medical devices is a plus

 - Knowledge in quality and regulatory affairs is a plus

 - You are perfectly fluent in French and English

 - You value teamwork and you are team-oriented. This role will interact with most internal teams. Moreover, happiness and well-being are a priority in the way we’re building the team and we also want to be innovative here!



Location: Paris

We move fast and aspire to be transparent over the process - our objective is that the process from the first chat to an offer is no longer than a month. We also aspire to give an answer to every application in a week - if you have not heard from us, please follow up at careers@sonio.ai.

source_jobs.title: HR Internship
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SONIO



Each year, 140M children are born. Birth defects affect 1 in 33 births in developed countries, and in 50% of cases, they are not detected during prenatal examinations. Prenatal medicine is particularly complex, and the scans impose heavy responsibilities on healthcare professionals. They can also be a source of stress for future parents.



Resulting from 5 years of collaboration between world-renowned experts from Necker Hospital and Ecole Polytechnique, Sonio uses artificial intelligence to improve prenatal screening and diagnosis. Based on patented algorithms and a proprietary expert database, Sonio aims to become the reference tool to help practitioners improve screening, reduce diagnostic errors, and optimize pregnant women’s medical path.



Sonio's mission is to improve women's and children's health by promoting access and quality of care through technological innovation, medical expertise, and collective intelligence.





About the role:



Being an HR at Sonio means enabling the team to thrive in a supportive, fulfilling, and creative environment. Our role is highly cross-functional to achieve this mission: recruitment, career management, performance management, administration, office management, and event organization.

The team is led by our Director of People & Culture, who works remotely, and we are looking for an intern to assist our HRBP in Paris with various HR tasks.

Your mission will be:

 - HR Administration: Daily management of employee matters, collection of payroll data, and management of administrative onboarding for hires.

 - Office Management: Be responsible for our office (necessary equipment and supplies, service providers, improving the quality of life at the office...)

 - Events: Support for organizing internal events (team-building, end-of-year party, etc.).

 - Support in recruitment: review applications, follow-up, intern recruitment campaigns...

 - Monitoring: research on HR topics to keep the team up to date.





About the profile:



Sonio is a mission-driven company, so interest in our mission is critical. Other requirements are:

 - Student in Bac+2 to Bac+5 (HR, Assistant, Management). Rigorous, organized, and excellent interpersonal skills are essential.

 - You have strong interpersonal skills: excellent verbal and written communication. Like Sonio's mission, you care about others.

 - You understand the confidentiality requirements related to our job,

 - Based in Paris, you speak English and French.



Location: Paris

Duration: September for 6 months

We move fast and aspire to be transparent over the process - our objective is that the process from the first chat to an offer is no longer than a month. We also aspire to give an answer to every application in a week - if you have not heard from us, please follow up at careers@sonio.ai.

source_jobs.title: Data Privacy Intern
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT SONIO



Each year, 140M children are born. Birth defects affect 1 in 33 births in developed countries, and in 50% of cases, they are not detected during prenatal examinations. Prenatal medicine is particularly complex, and the scans impose heavy responsibilities on healthcare professionals. They can also be a source of stress for future parents.



Resulting from 5 years of collaboration between world-renowned experts from Necker Hospital and Ecole Polytechnique, Sonio uses artificial intelligence to improve prenatal screening and diagnosis. Based on patented algorithms and a proprietary expert database, Sonio aims to become the reference tool to help practitioners improve screening, reduce diagnostic errors, and optimize pregnant women’s medical path.



Sonio's mission is to improve women's and children's health by promoting access and quality of care through technological innovation, medical expertise, and collective intelligence.





About the role:



If you are striving to learn new things and to discover the challenges that come with developing and bringing a medical device to market, our RAQA (Regulatory Affairs & Quality Assurance) team is looking for a Data Privacy Intern.

You are responsible for assisting in the development and monitoring of procedures to ensure that the organization’s health information is secure. You are also responsible for the management of all activities related to the development, implementation, maintenance of, and compliance with Sonio’s policies, procedures, and standards governing the privacy, confidentiality, and security in compliance with GDPR, HIPAA, and other state and federal laws protecting the confidentiality and privacy of individuals and their health information. Your duties include providing privacy and security training to the organization's staff who have access to electronic protected health information (ePHI), disseminating privacy and security policies and procedures, and planning for the timely resumption of access to information in the event of a serious disruption.



Key missions:

 - Assist management in the strategic planning of information privacy and security policies and procedures,

 - Performing Data Privacy Impact Assessment,

 - Maintaining register of Processing Activities,

 - Review for appropriateness of service or business contracts under which personal data processing activities are performed,

 - Participate in compliance projects to meet data privacy regulatory requirements,

 - Partner with the security team to review, enhance, and implement data privacy and security policies, processes, and training of the workforce,

 - Assesses ethics and compliance risks and implements localized compliance controls and other mitigating measures, as needed,

 - Ensures that compliance-related issues within the Company are appropriately evaluated, investigated, and resolved,

 - Disseminates information within the Company, raising awareness and conducting training for the workforce.





About the profile:



Sonio is a mission-driven company so interest in our mission is critical. Other requirements are:

 - Knowledge of GDPR and HIPAA regulations.

 - Knowledge and experience in information privacy laws.

 - Legal background

 - Project Management skills

 - Strong teamwork and communication skills, efficient and well-organized with excellent attention to detail

 - Proactive and able to lead subjects independently.

 - Fluent in English (written/oral)



Duration: asap for 6 months

Location: Paris

Salary: 1500€ per month

We move fast and aspire to be transparent over the process - our objective is that the process from the first chat to an offer is no longer than a month. We also aspire to give an answer to every application in a week - if you have not heard from us, please follow up at careers@sonio.ai.

notes: AI-focused prenatal screening and diagnosis company with a strong engineering core.
